obligations.description: An ADI must maintain a risk management framework that is appropriate to the nature, scale and complexity of its operations.
The Board must approve and oversee the risk management framework and ensure it remains adequate.
An ADI must identify, measure, evaluate, monitor, report and control or mitigate all material risks.
An ADI must maintain adequate capital to cover its credit, market and operational risk exposures.
An ADI must calculate its capital ratios in accordance with APRA's prescribed methodology.
A reporting entity must identify and verify the identity of customers before providing designated services.
A reporting entity must report suspicious matters to AUSTRAC as soon as practicable.
A reporting entity must conduct ongoing customer due diligence including transaction monitoring.
Transaction monitoring programs must be designed to detect unusual or suspicious patterns of activity.
An ADI must maintain an information security capability commensurate with the size and extent of threats to its information assets.
An ADI must classify its information assets by criticality and sensitivity.
